prompt: We expect cloud-services revenue to grow at a similar pace in fiscal 2027, though competitive pressure may compress margins.
Cloud-services revenue grew 18% year-over-year, reaching $4.2 billion in fiscal 2026.
A significant disruption to our cloud infrastructure providers could materially impair our ability to deliver services and harm our reputation.
Operating margin expanded 120 basis points in Q4 driven by leverage on fixed personnel costs.
Cybersecurity incidents continue to evolve, and despite our investments in detection, we cannot guarantee protection against all attack vectors.
Management intends to return at least 80% of free cash flow to shareholders via buybacks over the next three years.
Our gross margin for the year was 71.4%, down from 72.1% in the prior year, reflecting higher infrastructure costs.
We may not be able to retain key technical personnel in a labor market that remains tight for our skill requirements.
Looking ahead, we anticipate that AI-related infrastructure investments will weigh on near-term operating margins.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $912 million, or $2.04 per diluted share.
Foreign exchange volatility, particularly in markets exposed to the Japanese yen and euro, could adversely affect reported revenue.
We plan to expand into the Asia-Pacific region with two new data-center regions in the second half of the year.
